title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Patrick suggests that Miss Marple may have been killed and theorizes that Mr. Quincannon is the murderer, believing she never actually left Chipping Clegg. Letitia Blacklock defends Philip, arguing that he could not have killed Miss Muggleton because he was at the police station when the death occurred. Letitia becomes highly distressed, screaming about her fear of a killer, which prompts Patrick to offer her protection; however, Letitia receives this offer with disappointment.

Micky announces she will not be preparing dinner and retreats to her room, claiming she is frightened by the shadows and noises in the house. Julia takes over the cooking, insisting that Patrick taste every dish to ensure there are no accusations of poisoning. Following dinner, the group waits in the living room. Inspector Corrado calls to announce that he is arriving with Colonel Cadorick, his wife, and the Sweetnams.

In the kitchen, Julia argues with Midge over the use of cooking pans before heading to the front door to admit Miss Quincannon (also referred to as Miss Chicliff), who arrives looking relieved. The Cadoricks and Sweetnams arrive, contributing to a grave atmosphere. Constable Colquhoun begins an investigation, stating that the police believe the murderer of Miss Mulgrave is a woman. He asks the women to provide their whereabouts between 4:00 PM and 4:20 PM. Julia, appearing pale but calm, states that from 4:00 PM to 4:30 PM, she was walking along a field by a stream leading to the farmhouse at Compton, and she did not encounter anyone or approach the Gravel Pit House.
